text:
NEAR EAST - AFRICA

6. Shah may banish Qashqai khans:

The shah reportedly stated that he was assured by "other Qashqai chiefs" that the khans had lost the leadership of the tribes. He added that if they were exiled, it would not be necessary to use force against the Qashqai.

Comment: The unwillingness of the Qashqais to pledge their allegiance to the Zahedi government has greatly disturbed the government in the past months. An attempt to exile the khans might lead to a renewal of the Qashqai revolt against the government and necessitate large-scale military action against the tribe. The shah's present attitude indicates that he believes any emergency involving the tribes can be dealt with.

two chiefs of a Qashqai subtribe, apparently trying to increase their influence, are intriguing with local army commanders against the khans
